content:
Post 1

ASX 200 fell 20.10 points or 0.23% to 8,633.20, slipping below its 20-day moving average. The index has shed 0.61% over five sessions. A tech-led selloff drove the weakness, with IT stocks absorbing the heaviest selling pressure across the board.

Post 2

Information Technology dropped 2.24%, the session's worst sector. Energy led gains at +1.46%. AAI was the standout loser, falling 8.34% to AUD 93.90 — Alcoa's sharp decline dragged on materials sentiment. TUA topped the gainers, adding 5.88% to AUD 2.70.

Post 3

Gold is sitting at AUD 5,872.49 per oz with AUD/USD at 0.6994. A break below 0.6950 would amplify import cost pressures and weigh on rate-sensitive financials, which already lost 1.45% Thursday. Watch whether the AUD holds this level as US sessions develop overnight.
